get.description:
Check health of all application dependencies.

Returns 200 if all healthy, 503 if any dependency is down.
Initiate OIDC login by redirecting to the identity provider.
Handle OIDC callback after user authenticates with the identity provider.

post.description: Clear session and optionally trigger OIDC end_session.

get.description: Return current authenticated user info.
List all mail accounts for the current user.

schema.title: Response List Mail Accounts Api Mail Accounts Get

post.description:
Create a new mail account with encrypted credentials.

If the client does not supply ``polling_interval_minutes``, the
global ``default_polling_interval_minutes`` from user settings is
used instead of the hard-coded schema default.

get.description: Get a single mail account (credentials excluded).

put.description: Update a mail account. Only provided fields are updated.

post.description:
Test IMAP connectivity for a mail account.

Decrypts credentials just-in-time, connects, and reports results.

Reset a mail account's health status (clear circuit breaker).

Re-enables an account that was disabled due to consecutive errors.
Use this after fixing the underlying issue (e.g. corrected credentials,
restored network connectivity).

patch.description:
Update the pause state of a mail account.

When ``paused`` is true the account is paused — the scheduler will
skip it until it is unpaused.  When ``paused`` is false the pause
flag, error counters and the error history are cleared and the
scheduler is triggered immediately.

get.description:
List IMAP folders for a mail account.

Reuses the service-layer IMAP connection and folder listing to avoid
duplicating parsing logic. When ``counts=true``, includes per-folder
message and unseen counts (slower due to IMAP STATUS per folder).

post.description:
Manually trigger polling for a specific mail account.

Enqueues an ARQ task that polls the account for new UNSEEN messages
and feeds them into the AI processing pipeline.

delete.description:
Delete an IMAP folder on the mail server.

When ``move_to_inbox`` is True, all emails in the folder are moved
back to INBOX before the folder is deleted.  Otherwise emails in
the folder may be lost.

parameters.name: move_to_inbox

schema.description: Move all emails to INBOX before deleting the folder
schema.title: Move To Inbox

parameters.description: Move all emails to INBOX before deleting the folder

post.description: Rename/move an IMAP folder on the mail server.

put.description: Update the excluded folders list for a mail account.

get.description:
Get the current user's CardDAV configuration (credentials excluded).

Returns ``None`` (HTTP 200 with ``null`` body) when no config exists.
This is intentional: ``null`` tells the frontend "not configured yet"
and renders the setup form, whereas a 404 would trigger an error state.

schema.title: Response Get Config Api Contacts Config Get

put.description: Create or update CardDAV configuration with encrypted credentials.

post.description: Test a CardDAV connection before saving configuration.

Trigger a manual contact sync from CardDAV.

Uses the stored CardDAV configuration. Performs full re-sync
if no sync-token exists, otherwise incremental.

get.description: List cached contacts with optional search and pagination.

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Search by name, email, or organization
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Search by name, email, or organization

post.description:
Create a new local contact (not from CardDAV sync).

Generates a synthetic CardDAV UID for local-only contacts and
auto-assigns the email addresses to the new contact.
If CardDAV is configured, a write-back is attempted.

get.description:
List unique email addresses: senders from emails AND contact emails.

Returns every unique ``mail_from`` from ``email_summaries`` for this
user, together with the mail count and the contact ID if the sender
is already assigned to a contact.  Additionally includes all email
addresses stored in contacts that have never appeared as a sender
(with ``mail_count=0``), so the matching UI always shows the full
picture.

Use ``matched=false`` to get only unmatched senders,
``matched=true`` for only matched ones, or omit for all.

parameters.name: matched

schema.description: Filter by match status: true=matched, false=unmatched, omit=all
schema.title: Matched

parameters.description: Filter by match status: true=matched, false=unmatched, omit=all

schema.title: Response List All Senders Api Contacts Senders Get

post.description:
Use AI to extract contact information from all emails by a sender.

Collects all email summaries for the given sender address and asks the
AI to extract structured contact data (name, phone, organization, title).

get.description:
Get the AI-assigned contact for a specific mail.

Returns ``None`` (HTTP 200 with ``null`` body) when no assignment exists.

schema.title: Response Get Mail Contact Api Contacts Assignment  Account Id   Mail Uid  Get

get.description: Get a single cached contact by ID.
List mails associated with a contact via AI assignment (paginated).

delete.description: Remove an AI-assigned mail from a contact (delete the ContactAssignment row).

post.description:
Assign an email address to a contact.

Adds the email to the contact's ``emails`` JSON array (if not already
present) and triggers a CardDAV write-back to persist the change
in the upstream address book.  Also updates the Valkey cache so
subsequent matching resolves immediately.

delete.description:
Remove an email address from a contact.

Removes the email from the contact's ``emails`` JSON array and
triggers a CardDAV write-back to persist the change in the upstream
address book.  Also invalidates the Valkey cache entry.

get.description:
Get dashboard statistics for 24h / 7d / 30d time ranges.

Aggregates: mail processing actions, pending approvals, active accounts,
AI token usage, matched rules.

Get recent AI actions (label + folder changes), paginated.

Uses a SQL UNION with ORDER BY / OFFSET / LIMIT so the database
handles sorting and pagination instead of loading everything into memory.
Get recent account errors with timestamps, paginated in SQL.

Get ARQ job queue status from Valkey plus DB-based completion metrics.

Returns queue depth, in-progress jobs with mail UIDs, paginated
queued jobs, transient Valkey result counts, and persistent
DB-based completion/failure counters from ``TrackedEmail``.

Return status information for all ARQ cron jobs.

Reads ``cron:last_run:<name>`` keys from Valkey to determine
last execution time, and checks in-progress keys for running state.

post.description:
Manually enqueue a cron job for immediate execution.

Returns the ARQ job object's id on success.

get.description: Get failed mails from tracked_emails, paginated.

post.description: Retry a failed mail: set status back to pending and reset retry_count.
Dismiss a failed mail: mark as completed (user considers it handled).

get.description: List all AI providers for the current user.

schema.title: Response List Providers Api Ai Providers Get

post.description:
Create a new AI provider with encrypted API key.

When the user creates their first provider, all pipeline plugins
are auto-assigned to it so the pipeline works out of the box.

get.description: List all available plugins, sorted by user-defined order if set.

schema.title: Response List Plugins Api Ai Providers Plugins Get

get.description: Get a single AI provider (API key excluded).

put.description: Update an AI provider. Only provided fields are updated.

post.description: Test connectivity to an AI provider.

Reset an AI provider's health status (clear circuit breaker).

Re-enables a provider that was paused due to consecutive errors.
Use this after fixing the underlying issue (e.g. renewed API key,
restored network connectivity).

patch.description:
Update the pause state of an AI provider.

When ``paused`` is true the provider is paused — the scheduler will
skip it until it is unpaused.  When ``paused`` is false the pause
flag, error counters and the error history are cleared and the
scheduler is triggered immediately.

get.description:
List all prompts with defaults for each registered plugin.

Returns user-customized prompts where they exist, and default
prompts from the template files for the rest.

schema.title: Response List Prompts Api Prompts Get

get.description: List all available template variables with metadata.

schema.title: Response List Variables Api Prompts Variables Get

get.description:
Get the prompt for a specific AI function.

Returns the user-customized version if it exists, otherwise the default.

put.description: Create or update a custom prompt for an AI function.

post.description: Reset a prompt to its default template by deleting the custom version.

Render a prompt template with sample data for preview.

Uses realistic sample data enriched with the user's actual language
and timezone settings so the preview matches real pipeline output.

get.description: List approval queue entries for the current user.

post.description: Approve a pending AI action for execution.

Reject a pending AI action.

For spam rejections the email is re-queued through the AI pipeline
so that remaining plugins (labeling, smart_folder, etc.) still run.

patch.description:
Edit the proposed actions on a pending or manual_input approval.

Allows the user to override the AI-proposed actions before approving.
The edited_actions will be used instead of proposed_action when the
approval is executed.

delete.description:
Delete a resolved or expired approval entry.

Only non-pending approvals can be deleted to prevent accidental
removal of items that still need user action.

get.description:
Get the notification configuration for the current user.

Returns default empty config if none exists.
Apprise URLs are masked for security (they may contain tokens/passwords).

put.description:
Update notification configuration (templates and notify_on only).

Apprise URLs are managed via dedicated add/remove endpoints.

post.description: Add a new Apprise URL to the notification configuration.

delete.description: Remove an Apprise URL by index.

post.description: Send a test notification to all configured Apprise URLs.

get.description:
List all template variables available for notification templates.

Returns the 28 base email variables plus 13 notification-specific variables.

schema.title: Response List Variables Api Notifications Variables Get

get.description:
Return the default on-disk Jinja2 template for a given event type.

Falls back to the generic ``default.j2`` for event types without a
dedicated template file.

post.description: Preview a rendered notification template with sample data.

get.description:
Get CalDAV configuration (credentials excluded).

Returns ``None`` (HTTP 200 with ``null`` body) when no config exists.
This is intentional: ``null`` tells the frontend "not configured yet"
and renders the setup form, whereas a 404 would trigger an error state.

schema.title: Response Get Config Api Calendar Config Get

put.description:
Create or update CalDAV configuration.

Credentials are encrypted before storage.

post.description:
Test CalDAV connectivity.

Accepts credentials directly for pre-save testing.  Falls back to
stored configuration when no credentials are provided in the body.

get.description: List email summaries with pagination and optional filters.

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Text search across subject, sender, and summary
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Text search across subject, sender, and summary

get.description: Get a single email summary with full details.
List detected newsletters with pagination and optional sender filter.
Get a single detected newsletter with full details.
List extracted coupons with pagination and optional filters.
Get a single extracted coupon with full details.

patch.description: Update a coupon (e.g. mark as used).

get.description: List applied labels with pagination and optional label filter.
Get a summary of unique labels with usage counts.
List assigned folders with pagination and optional folder filter.
Get a summary of unique folders with usage counts.

delete.description:
Fully reset a smart folder.

1. For each mail account the user owns, connect via IMAP, move all
   messages in *folder_name* back to INBOX, then delete the folder.
2. Delete all ``assigned_folders`` records for this folder name.
3. Delete all ``folder_change_logs`` records for this folder name.
4. Reset ``tracked_emails`` entries for the affected mail UIDs back to
   ``pending`` so the next scheduler cycle re-processes them through the
   AI pipeline.

Returns a summary of what happened per account.

post.description:
Re-queue all emails in a folder for AI reprocessing.

Unlike ``reset_smart_folder``, this keeps the IMAP folder intact and
does **not** delete ``AssignedFolder`` or ``FolderChangeLog`` records.
Emails stay where they are; only their ``TrackedEmail`` status is set
back to ``QUEUED`` so the AI pipeline re-analyses them.

Works for both smart folders (looks up via ``AssignedFolder`` records)
and regular IMAP folders (falls back to ``TrackedEmail.current_folder``).

Useful when AI prompts or smart folder rules have changed and the
user wants to re-evaluate existing folder assignments without losing the
current folder structure.

get.description: List calendar events with pagination and optional search filter.
Get a single calendar event.

delete.description: Delete a calendar event record and remove it from CalDAV if synced.

post.description: Push an existing calendar event to CalDAV.

get.description: List auto-reply records with pagination and optional search filter.
Get a single auto-reply record.

put.description: Bulk update rule priorities for drag-drop reordering.

post.description:
Translate a natural language description into a structured rule.

Uses the user's default AI provider to generate the rule structure.
The result is NOT auto-saved -- the user must confirm and create via POST.

get.description: List all rules for the current user, ordered by priority.

post.description: Create a new processing rule.

get.description: Get a single rule by ID.

put.description: Update a rule. Only provided fields are changed.

post.description: Test a rule against sample mail data without executing actions.

get.description:
Get current user settings.

Returns default settings if none exist yet (auto-provisioned).

put.description: Update user settings (partial update -- only provided fields are changed).

post.description:
Report a mail as spam.

Moves the mail to the spam/junk folder and adds the sender
email + domain to the blocklist.

Report a contact as spam.

Blocks all email addresses associated with the contact and
deletes the contact record.

get.description: List blocklist entries with optional filtering and pagination.

parameters.name: entry_type

schema.description: Filter by type: email, domain, pattern
schema.title: Entry Type

post.description: Manually add a blocklist entry.

Test the AI processing pipeline with sample email data (SSE stream).

Streams progress events as each plugin step is processed:
  - ``step``: a plugin is about to be processed (index, total, name)
  - ``skip``: a plugin was skipped (with reason)
  - ``result``: a plugin finished (full PluginTestResult)
  - ``done``: pipeline completed (summary)
  - ``error``: fatal pipeline error

get.description: Serve index.html for client-side routing (SPA fallback).
